title: Design System: Churchill & Sejal's Dining Guide

title.purpose: Section headers, card names, modal/detail headings.

shadows.name: ambient-rest
shadows.purpose: Target at-rest elevation for cards and accordion sections (not yet implemented; current build is flat-at-rest).

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Back-to-top button, floats free over content.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default transition speed for hover/focus color and shadow changes across buttons, cards, filters.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Save Restaurant / main form submit action.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1F4E79; color: #fff; border: none; border-radius: 8px; padding: 9px 16px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; font-family: 'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #2E6DA4; }

components.name: Add Restaurant Pill
components.description: Header pill CTA to open the add-restaurant modal.
components.css: .ds-btn-pill { background: #ffffff; color: #1F4E79; border: none; border-radius: 20px; padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 12.5px; font-weight: 700; font-family: 'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-pill:hover { background: #D6E4F0; }

components.name: Filter Pill
components.description: Filter/toggle control; active state fills solid navy.
components.css: .ds-filter-pill { padding: 5px 14px; border-radius: 20px; border: 1.5px solid #d0d8e0; background: #ffffff; color: #444444; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; font-family: 'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s; } .ds-filter-pill:hover { border-color: #2E6DA4; color: #2E6DA4; } .ds-filter-pill--active { background: #1F4E79; color: #fff; border-color: #1F4E79; }

components.name: Dietary Badge
components.description: Small colored tag pairing an emoji glyph with a short label; vegan variant shown.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 7px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-badge--vegan { background: #E8F5E9; color: #2D6A4F; border: 1px solid #A5D6A7; }

components.name: Chase Badge
components.description: Fixed-brand badge for Chase Sapphire OpenTable credit eligibility.
components.css: .ds-chase-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 3px; background: #117ACA; color: #fff; border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 6px; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; font-family: 'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Restaurant Card
components.description: Primary content card; header strip tinted by city color (SF blue shown).
components.html:
Restaurant Name
Farm-to-Table · 123 Main St

What it's like, what to order.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 12px; border: 1.5px solid #d0d8e0; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.06); transition: box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.10); } .ds-card-header { background: #D6E4F0; padding: 13px 16px 10px; } .ds-card-name { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; color: #1a1a1a; } .ds-card-type { font-size: 11px; color: #666; margin-top: 2px; } .ds-card-body { padding: 12px 16px; } .ds-card-desc { font-size: 12.5px; color: #444; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Star Rating
components.description: Signature rating control; unfilled stars in border-gray, filled in amber. 44px min touch target on each glyph per WCAG 2.5.5.
components.html: ★★★★★
components.css: .ds-star-rating { display: inline-flex; gap: 2px; } .ds-star { font-size: 22px; line-height: 1; color: #d0d8e0; cursor: pointer; padding: 3px; border-radius: 4px; min-width: 28px; min-height: 28px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-star:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #2E6DA4; outline-offset: 1px; } .ds-star--filled { color: #f5b301; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Top search bar; pill-shaped, navy focus border.
components.html: <input class="ds-search-input" type="text" placeholder="🔍 Search restaurants by name, cuisine, or city…">
components.css: .ds-search-input { width: 100%; max-width: 480px; padding: 9px 16px; border-radius: 20px; border: 1.5px solid #d0d8e0; font-size: 13px; font-family: 'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif; outline: none; } .ds-search-input:focus { border-color: #2E6DA4; }

narrative.overview: This is a personal reference book, not a product marketing site: a deep-navy spine gives it the authority of a well-kept field guide, while a per-city color system — blue for San Francisco, green for Taos, clay-orange for Albuquerque — works like chapter tabs, letting the eye jump to the right section at a glance. Emoji badges (🌱🥩⚡📅🚶) are the annotations scribbled in the margins: fast, legible, a little playful, never decorative for its own sake. It explicitly rejects the enterprise-dashboard and generic-SaaS-list look. There is no audience to convert, no metrics to perform for — every visual decision should serve one question, asked standing in a parking lot: where do we eat?
keyCharacteristics: Deep navy as the authoritative spine color; per-city hues as wayfinding, not decoration.
Soft-lifted surfaces — cards read as physical index cards resting on the page, not flat rows in a data table.
Saturated, tactile fills on anything tappable; nothing ghost or outline-only.
Compact type at high information density, sized for scanning, not reading.

rules.name: The City-Tab Rule
rules.body: A city's color (SF blue / Taos green / ABQ clay) appears only on that city's section header, border, and heading text. It never bleeds into buttons, links, or global chrome — city color means "which chapter," nothing else.

rules.name: The Third-Party Color Rule
rules.body: External brand marks — OpenTable red (#DA3743), Resy black (#1A1A1A), Chase blue (#117ACA) — are reproduced as-given on their respective link buttons and badges. Never retint them to fit the app's palette; they're identity marks the user needs to recognize instantly.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Every text element uses the same system-sans stack. Don't introduce a second typeface for "hierarchy" — weight (400/500/600/700) and the four-step size scale are the only levers.

rules.name: The Rest-Isn't-Flat Rule
rules.body: Cards, the state accordion, and the detail panel always carry Ambient Rest elevation, even with nothing hovered. Flat-until-touched reads as a spreadsheet; this is a field guide.

dos: Do keep city color (SF blue / Taos green / ABQ clay) scoped to that city's own section per the City-Tab Rule.
Do reproduce OpenTable red, Resy black, and Chase blue exactly as their brand colors — they are identity marks, not part of this palette.
Do use saturated, confident fills on every tappable element (buttons, active filters, badges) — this is a fast-decision tool used on a phone, not a document to linger over.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion fallback, matching the existing skeleton shimmer's animation: none override.
Do keep the 44×44px minimum touch target on small interactive glyphs (star rating, close buttons), even when the visible icon is smaller.
donts: Don't introduce a second typeface. One system-sans family, weight and size carry hierarchy.
Don't let city color leak outside its own section into global chrome, buttons, or links.
Don't flatten cards back to zero elevation at rest — Ambient Rest applies even when nothing is hovered (The Rest-Isn't-Flat Rule).
Don't reach for enterprise-dashboard or generic-SaaS-list patterns: no dense data-table rows, no gray-on-gray sterility, no "this is trying to sell you something" polish. This has an audience of two.
Don't use uppercase tracked labels for emphasis; weight and color already carry it here.
